items.reason: All 20 decision areas listed in `docs/decisions/decisions-needed.md` (DEC-001 through DEC-020) have entries in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` with id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and non-empty projects_to. Required platform-shape decisions (DEC-016 dictionary direction, DEC-017 cross-spec reconciliation, DEC-018 global enums, DEC-019 closed privacy classes, DEC-020 migration direction) are all present with concrete projects_to lists.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` lists PEND-001 (target 2026-05-15) and PEND-005 (target 2026-05-03), each with owner (Andy), target_date, blocker, and rationale. Both target dates are in the future relative to research date 2026-04-24.

Each decision's consequences names concrete artifacts removed. DEC-001 removes 'per-function subject-to-person merge branches from supabase/functions/lti-launch-handler/index.ts...'; DEC-007 'Removes duplicate source-ID columns from every public table'; DEC-010 'Removes duplicated tenant where tenant_id = ... guard code from PostgREST callers'. Each names specific code paths or tables that the decision deletes or collapses, plus a 'Why not collapse' justification for preserving multiple surfaces.

Each decision lists three credible options including a more aggressive simplification. DEC-001 considers 'Collapse every user, actor, profile, and credential subject into one person' (aggressive); DEC-005 considers 'Use LTI AGS line items and results as the only canonical gradebook model' (aggressive consolidation). These are real architectural choices other production platforms make, not paper tigers.

`scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py --check` runs in VERIFY.md and the seed contains 58 objects/568 fields. However, the seed's `objects[]` array is keyed by spec_key with embedded `spec_object` blocks and per-spec canonical IDs like `canonical.oneroster-core.organization` — only ONE truly shared canonical object exists (`canonical.identity.person` in `canonical_objects`). The seed is a flattened union of per-spec dictionaries, not an upstream canonical model that projects to specs.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: lift the 58 spec-keyed objects into a top-level `canonical_objects` registry (currently only canonical.identity.person), and have the projections reference shared canonical objects rather than self-prefixed ones like `canonical.oneroster-core.organization`.

items.reason: Identity is unified at one place: `canonical.identity.person.email` projects to OneRoster `person.email`, LTI `lti_membership.email`, and Caliper `caliper_actor.identifier_value`. However, each per-spec field's own `canonical_field_id` points to a spec-prefixed ID (e.g., `canonical.oneroster-core.person.email`), not to the shared `canonical.identity.person.email`. The cross-spec overlap is documented in the canonical_objects registry but not enforced as the field-level canonical pointer the rubric requires.
items.traced_cause: dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email, dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor.identifier_value, dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.email: change canonical_field_id from spec-prefixed (e.g. canonical.oneroster-core.person.email) to the shared canonical.identity.person.email so each overlapping field points at the same canonical entry.

items.reason: `canonical.identity.person` exists in `canonical_objects` with fields (id, display_name, email, source_subject_id, identity_kind) and projections to OneRoster `person`, Caliper `caliper_actor`, LTI `lti_membership`, and LTI `lti_launch.subject_id`. OneRoster person and Caliper actor carry `canonical_object_id: canonical.identity.person`. However, QTI Candidate is not projected (QTI repository model has no Candidate object — DEC-012 says runtime is deferred), leaving identity unification incomplete.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json#canonical.identity.person.projections: add a QTI Candidate projection (or document its absence as a spec_only exception under DEC-012) so the canonical identity covers every identity-shaped object the rubric names (Person, User, Actor, Subject, Candidate).

items.reason: Per-spec dictionaries declare enums in two ways: inline `allowed_values` (e.g., OneRoster person.primary_role with student/teacher/admin/guardian) and `allowed_values_ref` strings like `base_status` and `true_false`. DEC-018 prescribes shared `shared_enum_id` references with bidirectional spec-native crosswalks, but no top-level `shared_enums` table with documented cross-spec value mappings exists in the seed. OneRoster role values, LTI URI roles in `lti_launch.roles`, and Caliper actor types are not mapped to a single shared enum (DEC-003 role-family vocabulary).
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add a top-level `shared_enums` table with bidirectional crosswalks (DEC-003 role family; status; identifier_type), and replace per-spec inline `allowed_values` on overlapping enums (OneRoster role, LTI roles, Caliper actor_type) with `shared_enum_id` references.

items.reason: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json#privacy_classes` lists nine closed values: public, directory, education_record, sensitive, credential, behavioral, operational, privacy_governance, system. `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` rejects `depends_on_contents` and `depends_on_entity` placeholders via `INVALID_PRIVACY_CLASSES` and validates every spec dictionary uses only values from the closed list.

`supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` is hand-written: it declares foreign keys (`references public.organizations(id)`), CHECK constraints, RLS policies, and views directly in SQL. The shared dictionary's object records do not declare `relationships` with FK targets and cardinality, and no generator produces migrations from the dictionary graph despite DEC-020 requiring this.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add a `relationships` array per object with FK source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Then write `scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py` that emits supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql from that graph and add a --check mode to VERIFY.md.

items.reason: Every field in `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json` and other per-spec files carries `decision_id` (e.g., person.email -> DEC-001-person-agent-subject; person.id -> DEC-007-identifier-crosswalk). `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` validates the decision_id references against the decision-trace block in standards-overlap-decisions.md and fails on broken links.

Per-spec dictionary pages (`docs/generated/*-dictionary.md`, `site/docs/*-dictionary.html`) and OpenAPI specs are generated by `scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py`, `generate_qti_core.py`, etc., and `check_dictionary_artifacts.py` verifies object/field/value coverage. However, hand-edited prose pages like `docs/lead-spec-accounting.md` and `docs/spec-gap-backlog.md` describe fields without per-claim links to dictionary anchors. No object/field/enum reference page generator exists at the platform level (only per-spec dictionary pages).
items.traced_cause: scripts/build_site_docs.py and docs/lead-spec-accounting.md: add per-field anchor links from prose claims (e.g. 'OneRoster Demographics fields') to generated dictionary anchors, and add a top-level object/field/enum reference page generator from data/data-dictionary.seed.json so cross-spec navigation does not require reading per-spec pages.

items.reason: `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` explains every decision (DEC-001 through DEC-020) in plain prose with question, options, choice, and consequences naming what is removed. The decision register is rendered as `docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html` and linked from `site/index.html` decisions section with cards summarizing each decision's choice and projected artifacts.

No `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` exists in the evidence. README.md lists many doc links but none walk through creating a school, enrolling students, posting a grade, linking a CASE standard, and reading a Caliper feed end-to-end with executable curl/SQL. `docs/loop-overrides.md` explicitly calls out this guide as required (priority 7) and it has not been written.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: write the missing end-to-end guide with copy-pasteable curl against live Supabase REST and Edge Function URLs (gradebook-bulk-submit, audited-roster-read, caliper-event-ingestion) for each of the five required steps, and link every field/enum reference to its dictionary anchor.

items.reason: Portal navigation (`site/index.html`) reaches dictionary pages, decisions, and OpenAPI pages. However, there is no automated link checker in VERIFY.md, no evidence of zero-404 verification, and no orphan check confirming every dictionary entry and decision is reachable from the landing page. Some referenced pages (e.g., `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md`) do not exist yet.
items.traced_cause: VERIFY.md and scripts/check_links.py (new): add an automated link checker over rendered site/ that confirms every dictionary object, every decision in standards-overlap-decisions.md, every API endpoint in openapi/generated/, and the buildability guide are reachable from site/index.html within three clicks with no 404s.

items.reason: A layperson reading only `docs/` cannot produce executable artifacts for the five required steps. No `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` exists. README links to `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json` (raw JSON, not docs) and `demo/README.md` (local-only), but no doc page explains how to create a school org with one teacher and ten students, enroll them, post a grade tied to the live runtime, link to a CASE URI (CASE is doc-only per DEC-012), or read a Caliper feed (only ingestion exists, no read endpoint).
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md plus the underlying gaps: (a) write the guide; (b) provide a tenant-create or tenant-bootstrap endpoint and document it (currently the seed tenant is hard-coded); (c) document a CASE URI lookup path (currently CASE has dictionary projection but no runtime per DEC-012); (d) implement and document a Caliper read/replay endpoint (only caliper-event-ingestion receipt path exists per DEC-012).

items.reason: `scripts/evaluate_platform.py` defines `DEFAULT_TRACED_CAUSES` for every rubric item, calls `ensure_traced_causes()` to fill non-pass verdicts, emits `traced_cause` strings naming lowest-layer artifacts (e.g., 'data/data-dictionary.seed.json and dictionary/*.v1.json: implement DEC-017...'), and emits a `buildability_gaps` array with traced causes. The output schema enforces non-empty traced_cause whenever status != pass.

`VERIFY.md` ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`. The script writes `done` flag, per-item status with traced_cause, and `buildability_gaps` array. `scripts/codex_loop.py` also calls `run_evaluator(..., label='before')` and `run_evaluator(..., label='after')` each iteration.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` defines `read_overrides()` to load `docs/loop-overrides.md`, `overrides_paused()` to detect a literal PAUSE token on its own line (excluding HTML-comment examples), and checks `if overrides_paused(overrides): ... return 0` BEFORE running the next iteration. `build_prompt()` accepts `overrides_text` and renders it as 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE — these supersede rubric defaults...)' inside the prompt.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` checks `if after_eval and after_eval.get('done'): print('Evaluator reports done after this iteration. Stopping the loop.'); return 0` and also `if before_eval and before_eval.get('done'): ... return 0` at iteration start. The hard cap `--max-iterations` (default 25) and `--stall-limit` (default 3) act as safety rails. No numeric-threshold gating; termination references the LLM evaluator's `done` flag.

buildability_gaps.gap: No documented endpoint or workflow for a layperson to create a tenant and a school organization. The runtime uses a hard-coded synthetic tenant ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111') and the seeded North Valley demo data; no docs explain how a new app developer would provision their own school org.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md (does not exist) plus a tenant/org bootstrap path: either document the supabase/seed.sql workflow as the layperson onboarding, or add an admin Edge Function for tenant-and-org creation and document it. Currently `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` only INSERTs are hand-managed.

buildability_gaps.gap: No doc walks a layperson from creating a course and class through enrolling a teacher and ten students. The Supabase REST `/rest/v1/classes` is read-only via RLS (`grant select` only); no documented insert path exists for app developers.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md plus supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql: extend RLS policies to allow tenant-scoped class/enrollment INSERT for authenticated users (or add an Edge Function), then document the full curl flow.

buildability_gaps.gap: The `gradebook-bulk-submit` Edge Function exists with a curl example in `docs/supabase-hosted-database.md`, but no doc walks through obtaining a `$SUPABASE_USER_JWT` for a teacher actor end-to-end. The buildability guide does not exist.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: walk through Supabase Auth signup, tenant-claim assignment, JWT retrieval, and gradebook-bulk-submit POST in one copy-pasteable sequence with field-level dictionary links.

buildability_gaps.gap: CASE is doc-only per DEC-012-runtime-coverage-per-spec. No live runtime endpoint exists to look up a CASE URI or attach it to a line item. The dictionary documents `case_item.uri` but no API serves it.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: DEC-012-runtime-coverage-per-spec: ship the deferred CASE search runtime slice (PEND-001-runtime-slice-schedule), and add a `case_alignment_id` or similar field on `line_items` in data/data-dictionary.seed.json plus the migration to store the alignment.

buildability_gaps.gap: Only a Caliper ingestion receipt path exists (`caliper-event-ingestion` Edge Function). No read/query endpoint serves a class activity feed back to the developer per DEC-012.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: DEC-012-runtime-coverage-per-spec: ship the deferred Caliper raw-event projection runtime slice and add a `/caliper-events?classId=...` Edge Function or PostgREST view, then document it in docs/build-an-edtech-app.md.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. QTI and CASE are doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated Edge Function receipt/governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, Caliper raw-event projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
